footnotes.text: The elements of the world: elementary principles of conduct, e.g., the religious laws of the Jews, and the various ceremonies of the heathens.

Crying, "Abba, Father": i.e., the Spirit teaches us, the adopted sons of God, to cry out: "Abba, Father." The two words are used because St. Paul, writing the Greek word, wished to preface it with the very word our Lord had used in this doctrine which was of such great importance.
Their feasts under the Jewish Law.
The Old Testament, and the synagogue.
The New Testament, and the Church.
